{{Quote|Lord Vader! What a great privilege to have you unexpectedly grace my humble domain.|Ghorin's last words to Darth Vader|audio=Ghorin01.flac|<ref name="SW:XW" />}}
'''Ghorin''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] who served as [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Overlord (Imperial)|overlord]] of the [[Greater Plooriod Cluster]] during the early [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. His territory contained several [[Agriworld/Legends|agricultural worlds]], and he was a major food supplier to the Empire. After the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Ghorin presented himself as sympathetic to the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] and offered to supply them with food. However, this was only part of a scheme intended to poison Rebel forces with tainted [[Grain/Legends|grain]]. The Rebels soon discovered Ghorin's plot and responded with a [[Operation Grain Snatch|plan]] designed to discredit the overlord in the eyes of the [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor]]. The operation was a success and Ghorin was assumed to be a traitor to the Empire. He was personally [[Death/Legends|executed]] by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] in his [[Ghorin's castle|castle]] on [[Plooriod III]].

==Biography==
===Imperial overlord and tainted grain===
A [[Human/Legends|Human]]<ref name="SW:XW" /> [[Gender/Legends|male]]<ref name="CSWE" /> who lived during the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]],<ref name="SW:XW" /> Ghorin was a strong supporter of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]],<ref name="CSWE" /> and, by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="NEC" /> had become the Imperial ruler of the [[Greater Plooriod Cluster]], an area of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] containing several [[Agriworld/Legends|agricultural worlds]] that served as major suppliers of [[Grain/Legends|grain]] to the Empire. Holding the title of [[Overlord (Imperial)|overlord]],<ref name="SW:XW" /> Ghorin ruled over his territory from a [[Ghorin's castle|vast castle]]<ref name="CSWE" /> overlooking the [[Ocean/Legends|ocean]] on<ref name="SW:XW" /> the [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="CSWE" /> [[Plooriod III]]. He soon earned a reputation as a ruthless and cunning ruler.<ref name="SW:XW" />

[[File:Plooriod3.png|thumb|left|250px|Ghorin's castle on Plooriod III]]
Shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], in which the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] destroyed the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]], an Imperial [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] with the [[Concave Dish Composite Beam Superlaser|power]] to destroy a planet,<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> the Rebels were forced to abandon their [[Great Temple/Legends|base]]<ref name="SW:XW" /> on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]]<ref name="CSWE Yavin 4">{{CSWECite|III|350-351|Yavin 4}}</ref> [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]]. However, losses during the escape from Yavin left them critically low on food supplies. In search of a new source of food, the Rebels launched a series of operations around the [[Ploo sector/Legends|Ploo sector]]. Learning of the Alliance's need, Ghorin devised a plan to poison many Rebels by supplying them with poisoned grain.<ref name="SW:XW" />

Claiming to have grown sympathetic to the Rebellion, Ghorin entered negotiations with the Rebels to supply them with grain and to potentially become a member of the Alliance. Though his reputation made the Rebels suspicious of him, their growing desperation left [[Ordnance and Supply/Legends|Ordnance and Supply Command]] with little choice other than to consider his offer. An initial shipment was arranged to be delivered at a transfer point near the edge of the Greater Plooriod Cluster, and Ghorin dispatched three [[BFF-1 bulk freighter/Legends|BFF-1 bulk freighters]] from ''[[Transmor]]'' group to deliver the tainted grain to the Rebels.<ref name="SW:XW" />

===Rebel revenge===
{{Quote|We received your grain shipments, Ghorin, and the Emperor has sent me here to personally repay you for your treachery.|Darth Vader|audio=GhorinVader-XW.flac|<ref name="SW:XW" />}}
The Rebels, however, remained suspicious of Ghorin's motives and conducted thorough tests of the grain before distributing it to their forces. When the tests revealed that Ghorin had double-crossed them, [[Alliance High Command/Legends|Alliance High Command]] devised a plan to get revenge on the overlord, codenamed [[Operation Grain Snatch]]. The [[CR90 corvette/Legends|CR90 corvette]] ''[[Halifax]]'' was delivering Ghorin's payment for a recent shipment of grain to the Empire, under escort from six of his [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|BTL Y-wing starfighters]]. Alliance [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] and [[Shuttle/Legends|shuttles]] were sent to intercept the convoy—Rebel Y-wings disabled the ''Halifax'' and its escorts, allowing the shuttles to board and capture Ghorin's Y-wings and to take his payment as compensation.<ref name="SW:XW" />

[[File:Ghorin.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Ghorin is visited by Darth Vader.]]
Having captured some of Ghorin's Y-wings, the Rebels planned to use them in a series of operations designed to discredit the overlord by convincing the Empire that Ghorin had betrayed them and not the Alliance. They first used the Y-wings to destroy grain supplies at an Imperial storage area in the [[Hollan sector/Legends|Hollan D1 sector]]. This was followed by a strike on a [[Geedon|convoy]] of CR90 corvettes delivering food to the [[Geedon system/Legends|Geedon system]]. Finally, the Rebels intercepted a convoy delivering grain from Ghorin to the Empire. After disabling the convoy craft, Rebel ships boarded them and replaced the crews. Furthermore, the convoy's cargo of grain was replaced with the tainted grain that Ghorin had previously supplied to the Alliance. The entire operation was timed to be concluded before the [[Imperial-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Badi Dea]]'' arrived, at which point the Rebels aboard the convoy craft completed the delivery of the tainted grain to the Empire.<ref name="SW:XW" />

The operation was a success and, after the poisoned grain was distributed to Imperial forces, Ghorin was presumed to be a traitor to the Empire. Upon hearing of Ghorin's apparent betrayal, Emperor Palpatine dispatched the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] to Plooriod III to confront him. Ghorin, unaware that he had been framed by the Rebels, greeted Vader in his throne room, but the Dark Lord wasted no time on explanations and promptly [[Death/Legends|executed]] the overlord.<ref name="SW:XW" /> Those events, with Ghorin mentioned by name, were later covered in the [[Historical Council|New Republic Historical Council]] document ''[[The Essential Chronology]]'',<ref name="EC">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> released just prior to the [[Yuuzhan Vong War]] of [[25 ABY/Legends|25 ABY]], and [[The New Essential Chronology (in-universe)|''The New Essential Chronology'']], an updated edition released in [[36 ABY]].<ref name="NEC non-death">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

==Personality and traits==
The [[Color/Legends|black]]-[[Hair/Legends|haired]], blue-[[Eye/Legends|eyed]] Ghorin was a corpulent individual with light skin who ruled over the Greater Plooriod Cluster from a vast castle on Plooriod III. He made the most of his opulent surroundings and would sit in a throne while he was fanned by [[Service droid/Legends|servant droids]]. The overlord had a reputation as a heavy-handed ruler and was likened by some to Emperor Palpatine. It was this reputation that led the Alliance to be suspicious of him and to ultimately discover his deception.<ref name="SW:XW" />

==Equipment==
Ghorin wore a purple dress [[Imperial military uniforms/Legends|uniform]], complete with epaulets.<ref name="SW:XW" /> 

==Behind the scenes==
Ghorin was created for ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Tour of Duty: Imperial Pursuit]]'', the first expansion pack to the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: X-Wing (video game)|''Star Wars: X-Wing'']], released in [[1994]].<ref name="SW:XW" /> ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'', published in [[2008]], had an entry for Ghorin which refers to his castle on the planet [[Plooriod IV]].<ref name="CSWE" /> This contradicts the ''Imperial Pursuit'' cut scene, which shows the castle on Plooriod III.<ref name="SW:XW" />
